steps: Open the 'Request Loan' module and open Loan_Request_Form
Select Loan_Type = 'Personal' (from Loan_Type options: Personal, Auto, Home)
Enter Loan_Amount = 10000 (within Personal range 1000-50000 as per constraint 'value must be between 1000 and 50000 when Loan_Type == Personal')
Enter Down_Payment_Amount = 2000 (satisfies 'value must be >= 0.10 * Loan_Amount' and 'value must be less than Loan_Amount')
Select Collateral_Account = 'Savings-3000' (account balance 3000 >= 0.20 * Loan_Amount = 2000, satisfying 'selected account balance must be >= 0.20 * Loan_Amount')
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Shows account details. (mock; no balance debits)

test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Shows account details. (mock; no balance debits)

The test must record which exact on_success/on_failure text is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_New_Password does not match New_Password (cross-field constraint)
steps: Enter a valid New_Password that satisfies strong-password policy
Enter a different value into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error displayed for Confirm_New_Password: 'must match New_Password'; validation errors highlight the appropriate fields

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_New_Password differs only by case (should fail match)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter Confirm_New_Password = 'newpass123!' (same characters, different case) into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: Fail: Confirm_New_Password must match New_Password — validation errors highlight Confirm_New_Password (references constraint: 'must match New_Password')

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_New_Password has leading/trailing whitespace (should fail exact match)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter Confirm_New_Password = ' Secure!234' (leading space) into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: Fail: Confirm_New_Password does not exactly match New_Password — validation errors highlight Confirm_New_Password (references constraint: 'must match New_Password')

test_cases.test_case: Pasted Current_Password with trailing whitespace (precondition mismatch)
steps: Paste the user's correct password plus a trailing space into Current_Password (e.g., 'CorrectPass ')
Enter a New_Password and matching Confirm_New_Password that would otherwise satisfy constraints
test_cases.expected_result: Fail: Current_Password must match the user's existing password — precondition not satisfied; validation errors highlight Current_Password (references precondition: 'Current_Password must match the user's existing password')

test_cases.test_case: New_Password identical to Current_Password (allowed if it meets strong policy)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter the same value into New_Password
Enter the same value into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: Pass if the chosen password satisfies 'must satisfy strong-password policy': on_success 'updates credentials and returns 'Password changed successfully.''; otherwise New_Password validation errors highlight New_Password (references constraint: 'must satisfy strong-password policy' and on_success text)

test_cases.test_case: Very long New_Password (5000 characters) — acceptance depends on strong-password policy
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter a 5000-character string into New_Password and the identical 5000-character string into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: If the 5000-char value satisfies 'must satisfy strong-password policy' then on_success 'updates credentials and returns 'Password changed successfully.''; otherwise validation errors highlight New_Password (references constraint: 'must satisfy strong-password policy' and on_success text)

test_cases.test_case: Unicode and emoji in New_Password and Confirm_New_Password (match + strength)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
test_cases.expected_result: If the Unicode/emoji value satisfies 'must satisfy strong-password policy' then on_success 'updates credentials and returns 'Password changed successfully.''; otherwise validation errors highlight New_Password (references constraint: 'must satisfy strong-password policy' and on_success text)

test_cases.test_case: Double-click the 'Change Password' button rapidly (should not produce duplicated success messages)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter a valid Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter a New_Password that satisfies 'must satisfy strong-password policy' into New_Password
Enter the identical value into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: UI shows a single on_success result: exactly one 'Password changed successfully.' message (references on_success text 'updates credentials and returns 'Password changed successfully.'') and no duplicated success messages

test_cases.test_case: Password manager autofills New_Password and Confirm_New_Password out-of-order before user interaction
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Allow a password manager/autofill to populate New_Password and Confirm_New_Password (fields become populated without manual typing)
test_cases.expected_result: If autofilled values satisfy 'must satisfy strong-password policy' and Confirm_New_Password matches New_Password, on_success 'updates credentials and returns 'Password changed successfully.''; otherwise validation errors highlight the appropriate field(s) (references constraint 'must satisfy strong-password policy', 'must match New_Password', and on_success text)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt second change immediately using the old Current_Password after a successful change (precondition changes)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Perform a successful password change: enter correct Current_Password, a valid New_Password and matching Confirm_New_Password, click 'Change Password' and observe on_success 'Password changed successfully.'
Immediately attempt another change using the original (now-previous) Current_Password as Current_Password and different New_Password/Confirm_New_Password values
test_cases.expected_result: Fail: second attempt should fail because Current_Password must match the user's existing password (after the first success the existing password has changed); validation errors highlight Current_Password (references precondition 'Current_Password must match the user's existing password' and on_success text from the first action)

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_New_Password uses different Unicode normalization form than New_Password (should fail exact match)
preconditions: Current_Password must match the user's existing password
steps: Enter the user's correct Current_Password into Current_Password
Enter New_Password containing a composed character (e.g., 'é' in NFC) into New_Password
Enter Confirm_New_Password containing the visually identical but differently normalized character (e.g., 'é' in NFD) into Confirm_New_Password
test_cases.expected_result: Fail: Confirm_New_Password must match New_Password exactly — validation errors highlight Confirm_New_Password (references constraint: 'must match New_Password')

steps: Do not attach any file to Attachment
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors displayed for required fields (at minimum Message_Body with 'must be present'); submission blocked and the form 'displays inline guidance' (Secure_Message_Form on_failure)

If the implementation permits this length, submission succeeds and shows 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID'.

preconditions: Message_Body is filled with valid text
steps: In Subject enter '    Short subject    ' (leading and trailing spaces)
test_cases.expected_result: The system's handling of whitespace is validated against 'subject length validated': if implementation trims whitespace and the trimmed length meets validation, submission succeeds with 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID'; otherwise, it fails and 'displays inline guidance'.

test_cases.test_case: Message_Body contains only whitespace (should fail due to 'must be present')
preconditions: Subject contains a valid short subject
steps: In Message_Body enter a string of spaces/newlines only

test_cases.test_case: Double-click 'Send Message' rapidly to check duplicate ticket creation
preconditions: Required fields (Message_Body) and Subject are filled with valid data
steps: Observe UI responses/messages after the second click
test_cases.expected_result: The UI should not create duplicate tickets. The user should see a single success outcome 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID' (one ticket ID displayed). If the second click triggers validation/failure, the UI should not produce two success messages. Duplicate ticket creation would be a failure of this test.

test_cases.test_case: Attach a zero-byte file of a valid type (exercises 'validates attachment types' without size info)
preconditions: Message_Body is filled and Subject provided
steps: Attach a zero-byte file whose extension corresponds to a typically allowed type (e.g., .pdf)
test_cases.expected_result: Because the AST specifies 'validates attachment types' (but not size), two acceptable outcomes are defined: if the attachment type validation passes despite zero-byte size, submission succeeds with 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID'; if the system rejects zero-byte files as invalid, it should 'display inline guidance'. The test verifies which behavior occurs and that the UI follows one consistent path.

test_cases.test_case: Clear the prefilled Phone_Number and submit Schedule_Callback_Form (should fail per 'phone number format validation')
preconditions: Preferred_Date set to a valid next business day
steps: Clear the prefilled Phone_Number field so it is empty
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL: Empty Phone_Number violates 'phone number format validation' and submission is blocked; the form shows validation errors inline ('validation errors shown inline').

test_cases.test_case: Preferred_Date set to next calendar day when that day is a weekend (should fail per 'must be at least the next business day')
preconditions: Test is executed on a day where the next calendar day is a weekend (e.g., today = Friday)
steps: Set Preferred_Date to the next calendar day which is a weekend (e.g., Saturday)
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL: Selecting the next calendar day when it is not a business day violates 'must be at least the next business day' and submission is blocked with 'validation errors shown inline'.

test_cases.test_case: After successful Send Message, use browser Back and attempt to send again without changing fields (verify duplicate prevention)
preconditions: User has successfully submitted Secure_Message_Form and observed 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID'
Browser retains previous form state when navigating back
steps: After seeing 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID', click browser Back to return to the filled Secure_Message_Form
Without changing fields, click 'Send Message' again
test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent unintended duplicate tickets. Either the second submission is blocked and the UI indicates the prior success (no new ticket created), or the UI displays a clear duplicate-handling message. At minimum, duplicate ticket creation is considered a failure. The original success message 'Message sent successfully; returns ticket ID' must remain a single authoritative result.
